abstract: As digital platforms increasingly rely on automated tools to govern user expression, an important practical question is whether algorithmic moderation can improve content quality without undermining user cooperation. Drawing on Wikipedia’s bot-based enforcement of neutrality rules, we find an unintended consequence: Contributors whose prior edits are moderated often respond with more politically slanted subsequent expression, rather than moving closer to neutrality. This pattern is stronger when moderation targets a contributor’s focal area of attention, among contributors with stronger prior political bias, and after repeated bot intervention. It is weaker when moderation occurs outside the contributor’s focal area and among contributors with greater experience in politically sensitive topics. Together, these findings suggest that effective platform governance requires more than scalable automated enforcement. For platform leaders, the results underscore the value of pairing bots with transparent explanations, context-sensitive messaging, and human oversight. For policymakers, the study indicates that algorithmic content governance should be evaluated not only by its ability to remove problematic content, but also by its downstream effects on user behavior, participation, and polarization. Well-designed governance systems must balance rule enforcement with users’ sense of autonomy.

affiliation: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
authors_full.name: Zhitao Yin

ob.ai_reason: 算法自动审核影响用户行为，AI为核心变量
ob.tldr: 研究基于维基百科机器人执行中立规则，发现算法审查会导致被审者后续编辑更倾向政治立场，尤其在针对其关注领域、已有政治偏见或重复干预时更明显，提示平台治理需结合透明解释、人工审查与用户自主感。

title: Psychological Reactance to the Algorithmic Management of Online Expressions
title_zh: 在线表达算法管理的心理抗拒
